steps.title: Build a tiny Desmos table
steps.content: The line passes through $(0,0)$ and has slope $51$. A slope of $51$ means that when $x$ goes from $0$ to $1$, $y$ goes from $0$ to $51$. Use the two points $(0,0)$ and $(1,51)$.

steps.title: Fit the linear model
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim mx_1+b$ in Desmos. The regression gives $m=51$ and $b=0$, so the line is $y=51x$.

steps.title: Choose the match
steps.content: The regression equation $y=51x$ matches choice A. The other choices have slopes $34$, $17$, and $1$, so none of them can have slope $51$. Choice A is correct.
